title.en: Reading the Spec: Frame Conventions, Optional Features and Channel Access
title.zh: 读懂规范：帧约定、可选特性与信道接入

blocks.en: Before IEEE Std 802.15.4-2024 tells you anything about Zigbee or Thread radios, it teaches you how to read its own pictures. Clause 4, 'Format conventions', is that rule book. It says how a data structure is drawn (Clause 4.1, General), how each field is sized and ordered (4.2, Fields), how a number is laid out bit-by-bit (4.3, Numbers), how a plain string of bits is laid out (4.4, Strings), in what order bits actually go on the air (4.5, Transmission order), and what to do with bits the spec has not assigned a meaning to yet (4.6, Reserved fields and values). Learn these six conventions once and every frame-format figure in the rest of the standard stops being a puzzle.
blocks.zh: 在 IEEE Std 802.15.4-2024 告诉你任何关于 Zigbee 或 Thread 无线电的内容之前，它先教你如何读懂它自己的图。第 4 章“格式约定”就是这本规则手册。它规定了数据结构如何绘制（第 4.1 节，总则）、每个字段如何确定大小与顺序（4.2，字段）、一个数字如何逐位排布（4.3，数字）、一串普通比特串如何排布（4.4，字符串）、比特实际上以什么顺序发上天线（4.5，传输顺序），以及对那些标准尚未赋予含义的比特该怎么处理（4.6，保留字段与取值）。把这六条约定学一遍，标准后面所有的帧格式图就不再是难题了。

blocks.en: The general convention (4.1) is that interfaces are described 'bit serial' — drawn as a stream of bits — even though a real chip might move 8 bits in parallel. The over-the-air bits can also differ once the PHY re-orders or encodes them, so these are interface drawings, not literal wire pictures. One hard rule: after a frame is fully expanded, it must be smaller than the maximum PHY service data unit (PSDU) the PHY in use can carry. A field (4.2) is one box in a figure, labelled with its size: '<Octets: n>' is n octets, '<Bits: m–n>' is bits m to n, and '<Octets: n/m/l>' means the box may be n, m, or l octets long. Fields read strictly left-to-right: the leftmost box is first, the next box is second, and so on.
blocks.zh: 总则约定（4.1）规定：接口以“比特串行”方式描述——画成一串比特流——即便真实芯片可能并行搬运 8 个比特。空口上的比特在 PHY 重新排序或编码后也可能不同，所以这些是接口示意图，而非字面意义上的线缆图。有一条硬性规则：一帧完全展开后，它必须小于所用 PHY 能承载的最大 PHY 服务数据单元（PSDU）。字段（4.2）就是图中的一个方框，并标注其大小：“<Octets: n>”表示 n 个字节，“<Bits: m–n>”表示第 m 到第 n 位，而“<Octets: n/m/l>”表示该框可以是 n、m 或 l 个字节长。字段严格从左到右读：最左边的框排第一，下一个框排第二，依此类推。

blocks.en: Numbers (4.3) and strings (4.4) look almost the same in a box but follow opposite intuitions. A number is written in the text as decimal nnn, binary 0bnnn, or hex 0xnnn, and unless told otherwise it is an unsigned integer. But inside a field the bits are drawn LSB leftmost, MSB rightmost — the reverse of how you write 0x47 on paper. So the value 0x47 sits in an 8-bit field as 1110 0010. For multi-octet numbers, the octet holding the least-significant bits comes first (leftmost). A string is just bits 0 to k–1 with bit 0 leftmost and no notion of significance at all. The takeaway: significance (LSB/MSB) only applies to things explicitly defined as numbers in 4.3; everything else is treated as a string.
blocks.zh: 数字（4.3）与字符串（4.4）在方框里看起来几乎一样，却遵循相反的直觉。数字在正文中写作十进制 nnn、二进制 0bnnn 或十六进制 0xnnn，且除非另有说明，否则它是无符号整数。但在字段内部，比特按“最低有效位（LSB）在最左、最高有效位（MSB）在最右”绘制——这与你在纸上写 0x47 的方式正好相反。于是取值 0x47 在一个 8 位字段中表现为 1110 0010。对于多字节数字，含最低有效位的那个字节排在最前（最左）。字符串则只是第 0 到第 k–1 位，第 0 位在最左，完全没有“有效性”这一概念。要点是：有效性（LSB/MSB）只适用于第 4.3 节里明确定义为数字的东西；其余一切都按字符串处理。

caption.en: Reading a number field per 4.3: the least significant bit is drawn leftmost and goes out first, which is why 0x47 appears as 1110 0010 — the visual reverse of how the hex value is written on paper.
caption.zh: 按第 4.3 节读懂数字字段：最低有效位画在最左、并最先发出，这就是为什么 0x47 表现为 1110 0010——与该十六进制值在纸上的写法正好视觉相反。

blocks.en: Transmission order (4.5) answers 'which bit leaves the antenna first?' For an address field (4.5.1), bits go out from the right-most octet (RMO) to the left-most octet (LMO), and within each octet from LSB to MSB. The same rule applies to a Vendor OUI/CID field (4.5.2) — the organisationally unique identifier that names a vendor. Finally, reserved fields and values (4.6) are the spec's safety valve for the future: every bit in a Reserved field shall be set to zero when you transmit, and shall be ignored when you receive. You must never make a decision based on the contents of a reserved field — that is how a 2024 device stays compatible with a feature added in some later amendment.
blocks.zh: 传输顺序（4.5）回答的是“哪个比特先离开天线？”对于地址字段（4.5.1），比特从最右字节（RMO）传到最左字节（LMO），且每个字节内部从 LSB 传到 MSB。同样的规则适用于厂商 OUI/CID 字段（4.5.2）——即标识某个厂商的“组织唯一标识符”。最后，保留字段与取值（4.6）是标准为未来留的安全阀：保留字段中的每一位在发送时都应置零，在接收时都应忽略。你绝不能基于保留字段的内容做出决策——一台 2024 年的设备正是这样与某个后续修订才加入的特性保持兼容的。

blocks.en: With the reading rules in hand, Clause 6, 'MAC functional description', covers the device behaviour that nearly every PAN shares. It opens with a refreshingly short clause 6.1, Optional features: in the base text everything is mandatory, and all the truly optional machinery (TSCH, DSME, low-energy modes, and so on) has been moved out to Clause 10. So Clause 6 is the common core. Clause 6.2, Device types and conventions, sets naming habits: constants are italic with an 'a' prefix (aBaseSlotDuration), PIB attributes — the MAC's stored settings, the 'PAN information base' — use a 'mac' prefix (macExtendedAddress) or 'phy' prefix. It also fixes the broadcast addresses: short broadcast and broadcast PAN ID are both 0xffff.
blocks.zh: 掌握了阅读规则后，第 6 章“MAC 功能描述”涵盖了几乎每个 PAN 都共用的设备行为。它以一段令人耳目一新的简短小节开篇——第 6.1 节“可选特性”：在基础正文里一切都是强制的，所有真正可选的机制（TSCH、DSME、低能耗模式等等）都被移到了第 10 章。所以第 6 章就是公共核心。第 6.2 节“设备类型与约定”确立了命名习惯：常量用斜体并加“a”前缀（aBaseSlotDuration），PIB 属性——即 MAC 存储的各项设置，“PAN 信息库”——用“mac”前缀（macExtendedAddress）或“phy”前缀。它还固定了广播地址：短广播地址与广播 PAN ID 都是 0xffff。

blocks.en: Channel access (6.3) is how a device politely takes a turn on the shared air. Two successive frames must be separated by an interframe space, or IFS (6.3.1): short frames are followed by a short IFS (SIFS), long frames by a long IFS (LIFS), and an acknowledgment is separated by the acknowledgment IFS (AIFS), with the ordering AIFS ≤ SIFS < LIFS so the radio has time to flip between transmit and receive. The random access method (6.3.2) is the CSMA-CA algorithm (carrier-sense multiple access with collision avoidance): a device counts down a random number of backoff periods, performs a clear channel assessment (CCA), and if the channel is busy it backs off again — increasing its backoff exponent BE up to macMaxBe and its attempt count NB until either the channel is idle (success) or NB exceeds macMaxCsmaBackoffs (failure).
blocks.zh: 信道接入（6.3）是设备在共享空口上礼貌地“轮流发言”的方式。两个连续的帧之间必须隔开一个帧间隔，即 IFS（6.3.1）：短帧后跟短帧间隔（SIFS），长帧后跟长帧间隔（LIFS），而确认帧之间隔开确认帧间隔（AIFS），其先后关系为 AIFS ≤ SIFS < LIFS，以便无线电有时间在发送与接收之间切换。随机接入方法（6.3.2）就是 CSMA-CA 算法（带冲突避免的载波侦听多址）：设备倒数随机个退避周期，执行一次空闲信道评估（CCA），若信道忙则再次退避——把退避指数 BE 增大至 macMaxBe、把尝试计数 NB 递增，直到信道空闲（成功），或 NB 超过 macMaxCsmaBackoffs（失败）。

blocks.en:
flowchart TD
  A[New transmission attempt] --> B[NB=0, BE=macMinBe]
  B --> C[Delay random backoff periods]
  C --> D[Perform CCA]
  D --> E{Channel idle?}
  E -- Yes --> F[Success: transmit frame]
  E -- No --> G[NB=NB+1, BE=min BE+1 macMaxBe]
  G --> H{NB > macMaxCsmaBackoffs?}
  H -- No --> C
  H -- Yes --> I[Channel access failure]
blocks.zh:
flowchart TD
  A[新的发送尝试] --> B[NB=0, BE=macMinBe]
  B --> C[随机退避若干周期]
  C --> D[执行 CCA]
  D --> E{信道空闲?}
  E -- 是 --> F[成功: 发送帧]
  E -- 否 --> G[NB=NB+1, BE=min BE+1 与 macMaxBe]
  G --> H{NB > macMaxCsmaBackoffs?}
  H -- 否 --> C
  H -- 是 --> I[信道接入失败]

blocks.en: Starting and maintaining PANs (6.4) is about birth and discovery of a network. Every device must be able to do a passive scan (6.4.1) — switch to each channel in turn, lowest number first, enable the receiver for a set ScanDuration, and record every unique Beacon frame it hears into a PAN descriptor (a beacon is unique if its PAN ID plus source address is new on that channel). Optionally a device can also do ED, orphan, active, and enhanced-active scans. To actually start a PAN (6.4.2), a coordinator first resets its MAC, runs an energy-detect (ED) scan and an active scan, picks a PAN ID that no neighbour is using, sets a short address below 0xffff, and then issues MLME-START.request. Synchronization (6.5) keeps everyone's clocks aligned: in beacon-enabled PANs you sync by decoding beacons (6.5.1); a device can ask for an Enhanced Beacon carrying specific information elements (6.5.2); and many primitives carry a Timestamp (6.5.3) taken from the local clock at a symbol boundary.
blocks.zh: 启动与维护 PAN（6.4）讲的是网络的诞生与发现。每台设备都必须能做被动扫描（6.4.1）——依次切到每个信道（从最小编号开始），把接收机开启一段设定的 ScanDuration，并把它听到的每一个唯一的信标帧记入一个 PAN 描述符（信标的 PAN ID 加源地址在该信道上是首次出现时即视为唯一）。设备还可以选做 ED、孤儿、主动以及增强主动扫描。要真正启动一个 PAN（6.4.2），协调器先复位它的 MAC，运行一次能量检测（ED）扫描和一次主动扫描，挑选一个邻居都没在用的 PAN ID，把短地址设为小于 0xffff 的值，然后发出 MLME-START.request。同步（6.5）让所有人的时钟保持一致：在启用信标的 PAN 里靠解码信标来同步（6.5.1）；设备可以请求一个携带特定信息元素的增强信标（6.5.2）；许多原语还携带一个在符号边界上由本地时钟取得的时间戳（6.5.3）。

blocks.en: Transmission, reception, and acknowledgment (6.6) is the heartbeat of every exchange. On the way out (6.6.1, Transmission), the MAC stamps each frame with a sequence number — a DSN for data and command frames, BSN for beacons, EBSN for enhanced beacons — fills in source and destination addresses (preferring a short address once associated), runs CSMA-CA, applies outgoing security, and only then sends. On the way in (6.6.2, Reception and rejection), the receiver discards any frame with a bad frame check sequence (FCS) and applies a list of acceptance filters — frame type and version not reserved, destination PAN ID and address matching — before passing the frame up. Acknowledgments and retransmissions (6.6.3) tie it together: a frame's acknowledgment-request (AR) field decides whether an Ack is expected; if it is and none arrives with the matching DSN, a direct transmission is retried up to macMaxFrameRetries times.
blocks.zh: 发送、接收与确认（6.6）是每一次交互的心跳。在发出方向（6.6.1，发送），MAC 给每帧打上一个序列号——数据帧和命令帧用 DSN、信标用 BSN、增强信标用 EBSN——填好源地址与目的地址（关联后优先使用短地址），运行 CSMA-CA，应用出站安全处理，然后才发送。在接收方向（6.6.2，接收与拒收），接收机丢弃任何帧校验序列（FCS）错误的帧，并在把帧上交之前套用一组接受过滤条件——帧类型与版本非保留值、目的 PAN ID 与地址匹配。确认与重传（6.6.3）把这一切串起来：帧的“需确认”（AR）字段决定是否期待一个 Ack；若期待却没收到带匹配 DSN 的 Ack，则直接发送会被重试至多 macMaxFrameRetries 次。

blocks.en: Notice how the conventions in Clause 4 quietly govern Clause 6. The DSN is an 8-bit number (4.3) that rolls over, which is why it only ties one Data frame to its immediate acknowledgment. The broadcast address 0xffff and the 'short address below 0xffff' rule all live in the unsigned-integer world of 4.3. And reserved frame types and versions (4.6) being rejected on receipt (6.6.2) is exactly the 'ignore on reception' promise that lets future amendments add frame types without breaking today's radios.
blocks.zh: 留意第 4 章的约定是如何悄悄支配第 6 章的。DSN 是一个会回卷的 8 位数字（4.3），这正是它只能把一个数据帧与其紧随的确认绑在一起的原因。广播地址 0xffff，以及“短地址小于 0xffff”这条规则，都活在第 4.3 节的无符号整数世界里。而保留的帧类型与版本（4.6）在接收时被拒收（6.6.2），正是那条“接收时忽略”的承诺——它让未来的修订能够添加新帧类型，而不会破坏今天的无线电。

summary.en: Coverage map: timing restrictions, guard time, and the three transmission scenarios
summary.zh: 覆盖地图：定时限制、保护时间，以及三种发送情形

blocks.en: Clause 4 conventions: 4.1 General (interfaces are bit-serial; expanded MAC frame must be < max PSDU). 4.2 Fields (<Octets:n>, <Bits:m–n>, <Octets:n/m/l>; leftmost field first). 4.3 Numbers (decimal/0b/0x; unsigned by default; LSB leftmost so 0x47 is 1110 0010; least-significant octet first). 4.4 Strings (bits 0..k–1, bit 0 leftmost, no significance). 4.5.1 Address field transmission order (RMO to LMO, LSB to MSB within octet). 4.5.2 Vendor OUI/CID transmission order (same RMO-to-LMO rule). 4.6 Reserved fields and values (set to zero on TX, ignore on RX, never decide on them). Clause 6 behaviour: 6.1 Optional features (base text all mandatory; options in Clause 10). 6.2 Device types and conventions (a-/mac-/phy- prefixes; broadcast = 0xffff). 6.3.1 IFS (SIFS/LIFS/AIFS, AIFS ≤ SIFS < LIFS). 6.3.2 Random access (CSMA-CA with NB, BE, macMinBe, macMaxBe, macMaxCsmaBackoffs). 6.4.1 Scanning (passive mandatory; lowest channel first; PAN descriptor). 6.4.2 Starting/realigning a PAN (reset, ED + active scan, pick PAN ID, MLME-START.request). 6.5.1 Synchronization general (beacon decode vs polling). 6.5.2 Requesting enhanced beacons (Enhanced Beacon Request, Enhanced Beacon Filter IE, Table 6-1 attribute→IE map). 6.5.3 Timestamps (local clock at symbol boundary; symbol periods or RSTU). 6.6.1 Transmission (DSN/BSN/EBSN sequence numbers, address selection, outgoing security). 6.6.2 Reception and rejection (FCS check, filtering rules a–e, Imm-Ack vs Enh-Ack). 6.6.3 Acknowledgments and retransmissions (AR field, AIFS timing, macMaxFrameRetries, same DSN on retry). 6.6.4 Transmission timing restrictions (allowed transmission interval / ATI; a frame plus its Ack plus a SIFS must finish before the ATI ends). 6.6.5 Guard time (MaxDrift = clock accuracy × time since the last synchronizing event; keeps adjacent ATIs from colliding). 6.6.6 Transmission scenarios (three outcomes: successful data transmission with Ack; lost Data frame — no Ack arrives, retry up to macMaxFrameRetries or hold in queue until macTransactionPersistenceTime; lost Ack frame — data arrived but Ack was missed, same retry/queue handling).
blocks.zh: 第 4 章约定：4.1 总则（接口为比特串行；展开后的 MAC 帧必须 < 最大 PSDU）。4.2 字段（<Octets:n>、<Bits:m–n>、<Octets:n/m/l>；最左字段在先）。4.3 数字（十进制/0b/0x；默认无符号；LSB 在最左，故 0x47 为 1110 0010；最低有效字节在先）。4.4 字符串（第 0..k–1 位，第 0 位在最左，无有效性概念）。4.5.1 地址字段传输顺序（从 RMO 到 LMO，字节内从 LSB 到 MSB）。4.5.2 厂商 OUI/CID 传输顺序（同样的 RMO 到 LMO 规则）。4.6 保留字段与取值（发送时置零、接收时忽略、绝不据其决策）。第 6 章行为：6.1 可选特性（基础正文全为强制；可选项在第 10 章）。6.2 设备类型与约定（a-/mac-/phy- 前缀；广播 = 0xffff）。6.3.1 帧间隔（SIFS/LIFS/AIFS，AIFS ≤ SIFS < LIFS）。6.3.2 随机接入（CSMA-CA，含 NB、BE、macMinBe、macMaxBe、macMaxCsmaBackoffs）。6.4.1 信道扫描（被动扫描强制；从最低信道起；PAN 描述符）。6.4.2 启动/重整 PAN（复位、ED + 主动扫描、选 PAN ID、MLME-START.request）。6.5.1 同步总则（解码信标对比轮询）。6.5.2 请求增强信标（增强信标请求、增强信标过滤 IE、表 6-1 属性→IE 映射）。6.5.3 时间戳（符号边界上的本地时钟；符号周期或 RSTU）。6.6.1 发送（DSN/BSN/EBSN 序列号、地址选择、出站安全）。6.6.2 接收与拒收（FCS 校验、过滤规则 a–e、Imm-Ack 对比 Enh-Ack）。6.6.3 确认与重传（AR 字段、AIFS 定时、macMaxFrameRetries、重传沿用同一 DSN）。6.6.4 发送定时限制（允许发送区间 / ATI；一帧加其 Ack 加一个 SIFS 必须在 ATI 结束前完成）。6.6.5 保护时间（MaxDrift = 时钟精度 × 距上次同步事件的时间；防止相邻 ATI 相撞）。6.6.6 发送情形（三种结果：带 Ack 的成功数据发送；数据帧丢失——收不到 Ack，直接发送重试至多 macMaxFrameRetries 次，或在队列中保留至 macTransactionPersistenceTime；Ack 帧丢失——数据到达但 Ack 丢失，按同样的重试/排队方式处理）。

cites.note: Clause 4 Format conventions — 4.1 General, 4.2 Fields, 4.3 Numbers (0x47 = 1110 0010), 4.4 Strings, 4.5.1 Address field transmission order, 4.5.2 Vendor OUI/CID transmission order, 4.6 Reserved fields and values

Clause 6 — 6.1 Optional features, 6.2 Device types and conventions, 6.3.1 IFS, 6.3.2 CSMA-CA, 6.4.1 Scanning/passive scan, 6.4.2 Starting a PAN, 6.5.1 sync, 6.5.2 enhanced beacons, 6.5.3 timestamps, 6.6.1 Transmission

Clause 6.6 — 6.6.2 Reception and rejection (FCS, filtering), 6.6.3 acknowledgments/retransmissions, 6.6.4 transmission timing restrictions (ATI), 6.6.5 guard time (MaxDrift), 6.6.6 transmission scenarios (successful / lost Data / lost Ack)
